name: Sniffle — BLE 4.x/5.x Sniffer (NCC Group)
label: Sniffle — BLE5 link-layer sniffer (TI CC13xx/CC26xx)
description: nccgroup/Sniffle — passive/active BLE 4.x/5.x link-layer sniffer for TI CC13xx/CC26xx. Follows connections, decodes extended advertising, and relays over Wireshark; also active scanning (TX scan-requests) and link-layer relay/MITM -> lab-only, authorized testing only. No jammer/deauth. CC flashes the firmware; the host runs Sniffle's own python scripts out-of-band.
note: Intel-HEX carries its own load addresses (app_offset not an esptool offset -> verify:). Do NOT set default_baud from the _1M asset suffix (that is the Sniffle HOST-comms runtime baud, not the flash baud); cc2538-bsl flash baud typ. 500000 -> keep verify:. Flash path: cc2538-bsl -p PORT --bootloader-sonoff-usb -ewv sniffle_cc1352p1_cc2652p1.hex (Sonoff); CatSniffer V3 via catnip_uploader/RP2040 bridge. TI LaunchPads are JTAG/UniFlash-only and OUT of cc2538_bsl scope -- do NOT claim CC flashes LaunchPads. FIRST shipped consumer of the new cc2538_bsl backend (bench-gated). danger lab-only (active scan + connection-follow + link-layer relay/MITM are active manipulation, like whad_butterfly; labelled, never blocked). Per-asset SHA-256 is in the API but kept verify: until confirmed on a real Sonoff CC2652P dongle.
